title: Indulgent Icy Grassy Indian Snappy Gulab Jamun
description: North Indian-inspired dumplings made with 00-flour and served in agave leaves, finished with a beurre-monte and a hint of grassy notes. This show-off dessert is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Combine 00 flour, chickpea flour, and almonds in a bowl. Mix well and set aside.
In a separate bowl, mix butter, ghee, and water until the butter is fully melted.
Add the agar agar powder and salt to the butter mixture and stir until dissolved.
Heat the mixture over medium heat and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Add the rose water and stir well.
Add the flour mixture to the butter mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and shape each piece into a small ball.
Heat ghee in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Fry the dumplings until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Drain the fried dumplings on paper towels and set aside.
To make the beurre monte, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form.
Add the icing sugar and whip until combined.
Serve the fried dumplings in agave leaves with a dollop of beurre monte on top.
Garnish with chopped almonds and a sprinkle of icing sugar.
Chill the dessert in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Serve chilled and enjoy!
Garnish with fresh rose petals and a sprinkle of icing sugar, if desired.
Store any leftover dessert in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Freeze the dessert for up to 2 months and thaw at room temperature when ready to serve.
